name: Local Coop
description: Multiple players share one device, session, camera model, and input space while cooperating in the same game flow.
design_notes: Local co-op avoids network latency but still has ownership problems: input devices, save profiles, camera framing, pause authority, UI focus, and join/drop behavior must be explicit. The design should define whether players share one camera, split the viewport, share inventory, or keep separate progression records.
design_purpose: Let players cooperate from one device without online accounts or matchmaking.
Create approachable social play for families, parties, and drop-in sessions.
Support shared-screen interaction where communication is immediate but screen and input focus are constrained.
player_fantasy: Players feel they can sit together, join quickly, and contribute without fighting the interface or camera.

parameters.name: local_player_index
parameters.description: Stable local seat used by input routing, UI prompts, and save profile binding.

parameters.name: input_device_id
parameters.description: Device or control partition assigned to a local player seat.

parameters.name: shared_camera_policy_id
parameters.description: Camera rule used when players separate, rejoin, or enter different rooms.

parameters.name: drop_in_join_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule for when a new local player can enter an active session.

parameters.name: save_profile_binding_id
parameters.description: Binding that determines which progression, achievements, and settings belong to the player.

parameters.name: shared_pause_owner_id
parameters.description: Policy for who can pause, unpause, open menus, or change shared settings.

edge_cases: A controller disconnects while its player is holding an item, aiming, or controlling a menu.
One player joins during a boss fight, cutscene, checkpoint load, or screen transition.
Two players open inventory, dialogue, or settings panels that compete for UI focus.
Shared camera bounds cannot keep both players visible without exposing hidden rooms or hazards.
common_bugs: Input is routed by device order instead of local_player_index and changes when controllers reconnect.
Guest players overwrite host settings, save data, or achievements because profile binding is implicit.
The camera teleports or zooms through walls when players separate vertically or enter different rooms.
Pause and menu state affects only one player while the other continues receiving gameplay input.
balancing_notes: Same-screen co-op encourages teamwork but needs level layouts that tolerate players moving at different skill levels.
Split screen gives independence at the cost of UI density and performance.
Drop-in rules should avoid spawning new players into immediate failure or blocking active teammates.
accessibility_notes: Allow each local player to remap controls, invert axes, adjust text scale, and choose assist settings independently where possible.
Provide camera and UI options that reduce motion discomfort in dynamic zoom or split-screen modes.

implementation_notes.unity: Use PlayerInputManager or an explicit local input registry keyed by local_player_index and input_device_id. Keep shared camera policy, pause ownership, and profile bindings in session state rather than in scene-only objects, and test controller reconnect paths.
implementation_notes.godot: Map input devices to player seats through a local session service. Player nodes should read local_player_index for UI prompts and save binding, while camera and pause services arbitrate shared state instead of each player opening menus independently.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use LocalPlayer instances, PlayerControllers, and split-screen settings deliberately; do not assume controller index is stable after reconnect. Store profile binding and shared pause policy in GameInstance or a local session subsystem.
implementation_notes.web: Use the Gamepad API and keyboard partitions through a local seat registry. Canvas or Phaser layouts should calculate camera and UI rectangles from shared_camera_policy_id and preserve per-player settings in local storage profiles.

relationships.reason: Local co-op often needs split-screen or shared-camera rules to give each player usable visibility.
Per-player local input bindings are needed when multiple devices share one machine.
Local co-op is a same-device variant of cooperative play with different authority and connection costs.

example_games.title: Overcooked
example_games.note: Uses local co-op around shared tasks, communication, and screen space.

example_games.title: Lovers in a Dangerous Spacetime
example_games.note: Uses same-screen local co-op with role movement between ship stations.
